title: $instancevalidation
text: $instancevalidation Identify whether data in the component instance requires validation. $instancevalidation { ( InstanceName ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description InstanceName String Name of component instance; optional. If omitted, the current instance is used. Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $instancevalidation Value Meaning "" An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. 0 No data has been modified. All the data has already been successfully validated. You can check the value of $instancemod to determine which of these situations applies. 1 Data requires validation. Table 3. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following this Function Value Error constant Meaning -57 <UACTERR_NO_INSTANCE> The named instance cannot be found in the component pool. -1101 <UPROCERR_FIELD> An incorrect field name was provided; either the field name is not valid syntactically or the field is not available in the component. -1105 <UPROCERR_INSTANCE> The instance name provided is not valid. For example, the argument contains incorrect characters. For more information, see newinstance . -1304 <UPROCERR_UNKNOWN_CONTEXT> Function not allowed, unknown context. The InstanceName argument was omitted and one of the following occurred: There is no current instance, for example, in the apStart trigger. The current instance is a form started with run . Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description The function $instancevalidation checks if any occurrence in the current component instance requires validation. This can be viewed as an inclusive OR of $occvalidation for all occurrences in the instance. Example: Checking the Validation Status of a Form The following example uses $instancevalidation to check the validation status of the current form: if ($instancevalidation = 1) message/info "Some data has not been validated." endif Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). $fieldvalidation Identify whether a field requires validation. $instancemod Return or set the modification status of data in the current component instance. $keyvalidation Identify whether a primary or candidate key requires validation. $occvalidation Check whether an occurrence requires validation.
